answers.supported_exploration: The most useful part for me as I was mostlz focusing on combat optimisation is easily sorting throuh damage types and ranges (the highlight feature) and easily seeing which spells are concentration spells so as to not put too many on one character. Also seeing the spread of damage types across the whole party helps diversify the damage types (or do the opposite and focus on one if I know im preparing for a specific zone or enocunter).
answers.changed_normal_planning: I dont usually use third party software as Im familiar enough with the game to not feel the need for one. This was nice as e.g. if I have some ideas for characters that I want to build and have somewhere without needing to use them in the game immediately (as in game you cannot have 'reserve' builds, you build a character and have to play with it), this is a nice 'idea storage'.
answers.first_change: Adding multiclassing would be nice as it is a feature in-game.
answers.blocked_exploration: No part of the tool felt like it was 'blocking' exploration. Because of how quick you can change a character I did not use the history feature much (rather than digging through the history I just go and change back whatever I need). I could see it being useful in a very long session or if I pause for a long time and have to remind myself what I was doing.
